title: TS-SatFire: A Multi-Task Satellite Image Time-Series Dataset for Wildfire Detection and Prediction
abstract_excerpt: TS-SatFire defines the primary dataset and benchmark setting behind the current AF, BA, and prediction workflows. It is the highest-priority reference because it establishes the task split design, input channel conventions, and baseline model families used by this repository.
key_findings: A single dataset can support active fire detection, burned area mapping, and next-day wildfire progression prediction.
Prediction quality benefits from auxiliary environmental data beyond satellite imagery alone.
The benchmark compares temporal, spatial, and spatial-temporal model families on the same dataset family.
useful_heuristics: Use TS-SatFire as the strongest source of truth for task definitions, split semantics, and variable conventions.
Prefer comparing spatial, temporal, and spatial-temporal model families before making stronger claims about improvements.
Treat auxiliary-feature ablations as high-value experiments for the prediction task.

reported_best_setup.note: See knowledge/papers/ts_satfire_summary.md for the curated summary and dataset-specific operational rules.

notes: Curated card derived from the local TS-SatFire summary. This should be treated as the highest-priority paper for experiment planning in this repository.
